scenery: Waitomo Glowworm Caves at Midnight New Zealand
outfit: A sleeveless midnight navy silk bias-cut slip dress with spaghetti straps and a mid-thigh hem, layered under a sheer ice-blue organza oversized blazer, finished with delicate strappy silver sandals.
hairstyle: Long straight jet-black hair center-parted and flowing loose past the shoulders with a wet-look gloss finish and wispy face-framing strands.
prompt: A young solo female model with Korean appearance sits reclined on a narrow wooden punt boat drifting through the Waitomo Glowworm Caves in New Zealand, leaning back on both palms with legs extended forward, head tilted upward in serene wonder at a ceiling alive with thousands of blue-green bioluminescent glowworms mirroring a galaxy above still black water. She wears a sleeveless midnight navy silk bias-cut slip dress layered under a sheer ice-blue organza oversized blazer, with strappy silver sandals, her long straight jet-black center-parted hair flowing loose with a wet-look gloss. The camera is positioned at water level, shooting upward at a low angle to capture both her silhouette and the glowing cave ceiling reflected symmetrically in the glassy dark river surface. Cool blue-green bioluminescent light bathes her skin with a soft ethereal glow, no harsh shadows, ambient light only sourced from the living ceiling. Shot in fine art portrait style with a wide-aperture prime lens, shallow depth of field blurring the far cave walls into velvet darkness, with subtle film grain adding an analog dreamlike quality to this otherworldly underground scene.
